title: oxydectes incana
intro: Oxydectes incana, also known as the "hairy woodsia," is a species of fern that belongs to the family Hymenophyllaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Oxydectes incana are numerous, with the plant being used to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The plant is prepared for herbal use through various methods, including infusion, decoction, and tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
